reviews.review_msg: Shauna is very egotistical for no reason, considering her pathetic credibility in her whole career. Her instructions are very broad, and horribly expressed. The content assigned are pretty useless and generic. Moreover, she is extremely rude towards students. She mentioned she has a stepson, which makes sense because who would ever want her?

Asks for feedback during lecture, takes notes and nods her head ("mhmm...") as if she is going to act on it. The fire alarm literally rang outside of our class and she says "well it's not ringing in our room" and continues to lecture. WHAT?!? She only stopped when another staff member opened our door and yelled at us to get out (true story)

Excellent professor who helps you learn and reflect on your life. The marking is tough and group marks make up a huge portion of your grades. One of the nicest people you will ever meet. Thank you Shauna!

The prof has severe ego issues.The course is based on participation and team work if you are having trouble with your team just BS your way though her assignments. Marking for assignments is very bad so always proactive approach to them (Ask prof or TA to explain HW ). Assignment are only one page but the requirements are too many
Literally just tie in course concepts from lecture, readings and videos in your individual and group assignments and your good to go. Assignment instructions are pretty confusing sometimes though.

Individual work consists of reflections and feedback which is honestly pretty easy if you just tie in course concepts. Same with group projects, youll have to meet with your group outside of class to finish the work(time consuming) honestly pretty easy, but very repetitive, and confusing assignment guidelines. Lots of group work. But Not that bad

Shauna is a terrible prof. She is very rude and unprofessional. The course is useless and yet she manages to make it really hard. She assigns a lot of homework but barely gives any time to complete it. Her expectations for assignments are never clear and the TAs mark very hard. If you care about your GPA don't take this course with Shauna.

Awful prof. Would not recommend her for any class. She marked hard, seemed rude, and gave so many assignments due in a week. The class is already useless but she makes it harder than it needs to be. The whole class is dependent on group work. It is hard to fail the class so that's a plus but if you can take it with another prof, I would.
Terrible prof, agree with all the other comments. Easy class, prof didnt teach me anything and was not respectful.

I will be straight. Is this course challenging? Yes. Is Shauna a good prof? Yes. The ratings on here equate poor performances in BUS202 to a professor problem but that is not the case. Shauna is caring, creates an inclusive environment, and there for u. It is the students responsibility to take this class seriously and find value in it. I did.

Shauna Jones is by far, the worst prof I ever had at SFU. Seeing that BUS 202 is a mandatory course in your first semester in Beedie, I truly don't know if SFU wants to continue having her as the first Beedie professor students encounter when they get accepted into the school of business. Do it with ANY other prof than her.
not respect to students

Shauna is very nice and pretty knowledgable in general. My complaint is that she is sometimes unclear about what she wants to see on assignments and sometimes contradicts herself, or has hidden expectations that aren't on the criteria.
The worst prof I've every had during my four-year studies in Beedie. She is a horrible person and has no respect for students.

I like the intention of this course but there is a lack of focus on the course material. I still don't get why the "Core Strength" test is placed with so much focus, it is just some personality test that is true to some extent. Stop treating it as some "solution for all things" test and instead give students more time to focus on the BPME project.

Avoid at all costs. I did not heed the warnings of those before me and paid the price. By far the worst experience I've ever had in a class at SFU and I can take a lot. Take this class with someone else I cannot warn you enough.
worst course ever. not a good prof as well.

The worst course forever. Grading is not based on curve/cut-off but only by her impression to your team. How come 79.81% can get a C+ but 80.5% can have a B? Ridiculous grading and SFU should cut this course out/make it into a non-credit course. The task team interview is the time to determine her impression to your team. Bad teama bad grade

Complaining about this class is similar to that of stating your own incompetence. I'm as lazy as can be. However, I managed to do well in Shauna's class by listening to what she wanted and understanding the TA's needs. 360W is a course where you can succeed with minimal effort as long as you understand what needs to be done. A very pleasant class.

Shauna's classes contain a lot of "fluff". Showing up is mandatory and the expectations can be very unclear from the get-go. Group work makes a huge portion of your grade. Communicate with your TA religiously, especially with your reflections. The class itself is pretty useless but if you have a decent group and manage your time it's an easy grade

The real life case is painful. We are supposed to analyse a company's problem and give them a recommendation. Employees has their degrees, and they reply on the company for income. If they can't come up with useful advises to solve the company's problem, how well do you think you are gonna make a good recommendation? And how well can your grade be?

Strongly recommend you to take someone else's class. She expects you read your whole chapter before class but the lecture is about something else, i.e. the book is kinda not covered in the lecture. She always bring life example into this course which makes it really difficult and massive of work. And these make it harder than it should be.

Most unpredictable teacher  I ever had. Disorganized, and chaos are the best describe of her summit.  She tells you to grade you peers on the true performance, and then she grades down what you receive because of 'over-inflation'. However, when you take a look at the average, you discover it is higher than what you get. Wouldn't recommend her ever.

This class was an incredible amount of work. Large percentage of mark is group work. If your grade is more important to you than most students, be prepared to do most of the work yourself. Our group project was a pilot use of Riipen, which was hard because it didn't align with the  assignment well. Hardest I've worked for any class.

If you want a good grade in this course, you need a good group with people who care. You can't do anything but hope for the best. It sucks because I was put in a bad group with most people who didn't care. This course has many flaws.

Pretty disorganized. Assignment outlines are vague and full of overlaps. Expects a ton of detail but sets very restricting word/page limits. Tells students not to worry about taking notes cause she'll post slides on Canvas, but then doesn't do so. Also tells students that their grades don't matter, which is kind of ridiculous. At least she's nice?

Shauna is really nice and is willing to help her students. My only problem with this class was with the administration. The criteria and guidelines were not clear and were up late in some cases. Most important tip in this class is to be very specific and elaborate on what you're saying in order to be perfectly clear.
Shauna is probably the best prof to take for 360W. Assignments are clear, and her marking is very fair. You will get A+'s in her class as long as your writing is up to standard!

If you have to take BUS360, take it with Shauna. Although the workload is difficult, she is a great professor who cares and wants you to improve your writing. She gives lots of helpful feedback, is very organized with her rubric, and tends to give less portfolio assignment that other 360 profs.

Shauna's a great professor always willing to help and overall great prof. She is a fair marker and if you get a bad grade, blame that on the course being tough. Put in the time and effort and you'll see results.
She made my first term at sfu great, thank you!
The curve is your friend 3 I'm not sure how she does it but your grades will always be higher than what you get

Made my first experience at SFU great. This was a required course and I'll be honest, I was expecting it to be tough. However, the easy and simplicity brought to us through her lectures was extraordinary. Please take her, you won't regret it.
fair grader, super helpful, took for 202, managed an A in her class

She is helpful. Marking is a bit skewed; no standard marking scheme across the board. Textbook necessary for readings. She curves down if average too high. This class wasnt taught as clean as the intention to incorporate collaboration through marketing & selling a product together. SO MUCH WRITING.

This class is so much reflection you have to invent stuff. Shauna's marking is the toughest thing I've ever seen.  She doesn't back up her grading with valid statements and to be told you failed at collaborating is a joke because that is a matter of opinion. If you are an independent worker who cares about your GPA this class will infuriate you.
very tough, but you learn a lot from her. easily the most applicable class taken in the business major.

Shauna is nice and willing to help you. But the criteria for marking is wired, sometimes you will think it's unfair because she will give high grade to what she has expected, formation is not very important. If you have questions please to ask professor because TA won't tell anything
She is not a good professor, and not fair in this class.

A course intended to help students though, but requirements are not so clear, and the grades they give you are mostly subjective, I learnt something but not from the instructor, she seem not that professional to teach students, and the textbook is useless don't know why they asked us to buy it.
What efforts you put in = what grade you will receive. Work hard and it will pay back! She is trying her best to help you improve your writing skills.
Shauna is very very bad

Shauna knows what she's doing. This isn't a course where you memorize facts. You can't be lazy and have to work. It's a practical course and not theoretical (which doesn't teach you anything in business). I had to work hard, but at least I learned something useful.

If you are ESL students,AVOID her!Don't expect her to help you improve your writing because she makes tons of mistakes.Doesn't care about her job and students.She's inconsistent with her marking.Cocky,impatient,and unprofessional. Ironic to have a prof like this to teach business communications.SFU HR department's epic fail!

A real business course compared to the courses where you memorize the textbook. Must work hard every week if you want to do well. Make sure to use resources effectively. (ie. TA, writing mentors, library)

Teaching notes are not specific enough. Need to see more samples or detail on teaching slides to complete assignments accurately. You get marked down for too little detail and sometimes for too much detail - what are you supposed to do?  Low class average (no surprise there). Ambiguous assignments with incomplete information.
Hard and picky marker; agree with unclear guidelines;improvement needed re: communication sills; not learning how to be a better writer which is the intent of the course but being nit-picked instead

Tough class no matter who you take it with. Do exactly what she says and give her exactly what she wants/asks for down to the smallest detail and will do fine. You do not know better than her. Pretending you do = fail.
Her grading for assignments usually starts at A-. In general, fair grading and very nice.

Take the old reviews of her with a grain of salt. Im pretty sure she's read these things and changed stuff. She is SUPER nice! I would recommend her! The reality of the situation is that you DO have to put the effort into this class...she honestly is very helpful and if you want a good grade you do need to see her or a TA for revisions.

MAKE FRIENDS WITH YOUR TA! TA office hours are a lifesave for this course, Shauna is a TOUGH grader and very inconsistent. Nit-picky, and plays favorites so you might want to speak up in class. Also, put some effort in, as ridiculous as she is sometimes, the abilities you can gain are actually important (more so then any other course I've taken).
Shauna is a great professor if you want to learn how useful a class will be for you.
Horrible prof, stay away! Her marking is inconsistent and picky as hell. Honestly, she talks about her "success" 80% of the time. Teach us, don't brag. If you want to fail, take this prof.

Where do I start? Plays favourites with the hardcore suck ups. Doesn't know jack about the whole business process. If you want to be a professional secretary, then you should take 360 with Shauna. Oh, how did I almost forget the insanely unclear guidelines that goes with every assignment? Doesn't know how to weigh assignments either.
Too useless lectures, wasting more than half of the class time doing useless activities. Never teaches student how should write but expects students to figure out by reading textbook.
she is a nice prof!
Shauna is very helpful prof! I would definitely recommend her! YOu can learn a lot from her!!!

An absolutley horible prof!!! Quite possibly the most useless person ever. An extremely poor attitude towards students, excluding the few that were willing to put countless hours into sucking up. She goes on about writing business reports for clients; if she is so good at her side job maybe she could spare us her egalitarianism and quit teaching
No Comments
Shauna is a great lecturer! She is very helpful & wants everyone to do well. itz just a matter of ur work effort and quality to c if u can make the grade. Definitely recommended!

Shauna is a great speaker. She makes lectures interactive & FUN which is a huge improvement over most BUS classes. Guidelines are a little unclear, so make sure you clarify with her, and GO TO OFFICE HOURS--She definitely "favours" the people who make an effort!
she is nice!
She's not as bad as everyone said, just be nice to her and she'll be nice to you...
she is good.. strong communication skills

AVOID! Disorganized and sloppy herself but expects absolute professionalism from students. Treats students like children (ie: rings bell to get attention). Very subjective marker with little usable feedback: even if you correct your errors, your marks don't improve as she will just find more errors. Recomm taking anyone else. Not worth the headache

She marks just as hard as Stuart, so don't take it with her to avoid him. Unorganized, instructions are cryptive. Can't take any negative feedback or suggestions. Not recommended. Will lecture you on how to dress professionally, but doesn't have a clue herself. Avoid this one.
She is a decent prof. The course does require ALOT of work, but is usefull. She could coordinate this course a lot better if she did it on her own, and not try to follow stewart.

She blames the students for not reading the instructions when it's her own instructions which aren't clear. When half the class doesn't get the instructions, it usually isn't the students' fault. She marks incredibly hard and subjective. Never know when you're too wordy or not enough detail. Patient was much better when he guess lectured.

Nice teacher, but is not organized. To do well, must revise a lot & must see the TA/Shauna for feedbacks. This is a SUBJECTIVE course; different ppl read your work differently, thus, it just not how good you write, it's who reads it and how it affects them. She does make a lot of typos in her handouts. It's ironic b/c this a communication course.

Com'on..she is TERRIBLE. talks like a highschool teacher in class but a bossy b*tch in office hours. Doesn't teach much but requires A LOT from you. Unclear of what she wants. Always making changes. Very FAKE. the TA is better than her... Expects too much from students when she herself is a very disorganized, last-minute person who makes lots typo

what are you guys talking about? she's the most disorganized prof I have Ever had.  Not to mention utterly boring, a horribly hard marker, and basically cocky, with a very 'impersonal tone'.  Marking, as mentioned, is extremely difficult, and she likes things a certain very SPECIFIC way. if you do anything else, you will be harshly penalized.
Great prof. Really enthusiastic with her work. Best advice is to revise your work EARLY. go see the TA or the Prof regularly for your revisions.

Well, this is a course which you have to put in your best effort. Marking is tough, so prepare to get some disappointments. Although the class got 3 outlines, shauna is helpful and clear. This course is not as easy as i thought but it's very practical.

Easy course if you have the time to dedicate to it. Always do your revisions on assignments. Course was slightly disorganized, but is practical and full of material that you'll probably one day have to use.

Great instructor even though she had to make many changes to the course. Business Communication isn't easy so you can't expect to get many A's. She's way better than Patient (had him as a sub 2 times) and Stewart (my friends had him)... so, I recommend taking BUS 360 with Jones.
there must have been 10 administrative changes, 3 course outlines, changed readings, changes to assignments. marking was relative to earlier performance and seemed cooked to help ESL students.
No Comments
